title: Podoluma gardneri
intro: Podoluma gardneri, also known as Plectranthus ciliatus, is a flowering plant species within the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Plectranthus ciliatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, such as rosmarinic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Plectranthus ciliatus is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular herbal preparations among those seeking to harness its potential health benefits.
